def: s.f. See s.v. kāw.
s.f. A kind of slipper (without heels, the back part being flattened down under the heel); a slipper, shoe.
s.m. (dialec.), A table-decker; sewer (cf. bakāwal).

ety: Prk. काअत्रो; S. काक+कः; cf. also S. कारवः
def: s.m. (f. -ī) A crow
subs: s.f. The flower of Clitoria ternatea
(S. kāka+tuṇḍī), s.f. A species of Agallochum; — the plant Abrus precatorius
s.f. lit. 'One who scares crows'; a disgraced woman; a woman set to mean offices
lit. 'Crow's starch'; a kind of arrowroot.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi کَوا (kau(v)ā /⁠kvā⁠/) (c. 1609), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀸𑀬 (kāya), from Sanskrit काक (kā́ka). Cognates Compare Kashmiri کاو (kāv), Punjabi کاں (kāṉ) and کَن٘و (kaṉv), Sindhi ڪَان (kā̃) and ڪَاءُن (kā̃ũ), Shina کاں (kā̃), Bengali কাউয়া (kauẏa). Doublet of کاگ (kāg).

ety: intens. n. fr. قول 'to say; to tell,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Speaking much, or well; loquacious; fluent, eloquent; — one who speaks well, or fluently, &c.; — a professional story-teller; — a kind of musician who sings, and plays (on the sārangī, or t̤ambūra, &c.); a singer.
s.f. Singing and playing (esp. for dancing dervishes).
s.f. A female singer and musician (see qawwāl).
s.m. A poem, poetical composition; — a bard; — a patron
subs: vulg. kāvya-ćor, s.m. A robber of other poems, a plagiarist
s.m. Taste for poetical composition; — the flavour or sweetness of poetry.

def: s.m. Oblation or offering of food to deceased ancestors.
s.m. (dialec.) = kāya, q.v.
s.f. The body; appearance; person
subs: s.m. 'Change of body'; the recovering freshness and healthiness of appearance (after sickness); an elixir or aphrodisiac for restoring the old to youth; — transmigration
(apnī), To recover freshness and healthiness of look (after sickness); to undergo transmigration, to transmigrate
s.m. Bodily pain, bodily toil or labour
s.m. Restoring youth by means of certain medicines, &c. (see kāyā-palaṭ).

def: s.m. The body, &c. (see kāyā); — the root of the little finger; — a nuptial form called Prājāpatya.
adj. (f. -ikā, or -ikī), Belonging or relating to the body, corporeal, bodily, personal.
s.f. The cajeput (from whence is prepared the well-known oil).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Cowardly, pusillanimous, timid, timorous; — a coward, &c.; — name of a bird, a kind of thrush (see sāt-bhāʼī).
adj. = kāyar, q.v.

s.m. (f. -ā), lit. 'Situated in the body, incorporate'; the Supreme Being or Spirit; — name of a mixed tribe or caste of Hindūs (sprung from a Kshatriya father and Śūdra mother), the writer caste; a man of that caste, a writer, scribe, clerk.
s.m. The writer caste of Hindūs, &c. ( = kāyasth).
s.f. The modified form of the Devanāgarī character which is used by Kāyaths or (Hindū) scribes (esp. in and about Patna).
adj. (f. -inī) = kāyak, q.v.

s.m. The small prickly tree, Fragaria vesca, the bark and seeds of which are used as aromatics; the aromatic bark of the Fragaria vesca (P. syn. dār-ě-shīshaʻān); — Kiliorum bark, or wild nutmeg, Myrica sapida.
s.m. & f. A goose, a duck
s.m. Raw silk; silk.

ety: inf. n. of قضی 'to decree; to appoint,' &c.
def: s.f. Divine decree, predestination; fate, destiny; fatality; death; decree, mandate, judgment, order, charge, edict; office, or sentence (of a judge); judicature; jurisdiction, diocese; administration of justice; performance, fulfilment (of a duty, &c.); prayer repeated at the appointed time; — saying a prayer after the time appointed for it is passed; making up for the omission of an appointed duty (as a prayer, or a fast)
subs: or qaẓā bharnā (-kī), To make up for the omission (of an appointed religious duty)
To pray again, or to say extra prayers (in order to make up for a former omission)
s.f. Performance of a work of necessity, going to the necessary
s.f. Repeating extra prayers to make up for prayers omitted in the former part of one's life; extra prayers
or qaẓā-rā, or qaẓā 'indaʼl-lāh, or qaẓā liʼl-lāh, adv. 'By the operation of the divine decree, or by the action of fate'; providentially; by chance; it so happened that
(-kī), To omit the performance (of a religious duty); to make up for a former omission (of a religious duty); — to die
s.f. Fate; predestination; the angels who preside over destiny, the recording angels.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَضَاء (qazā'), from Arabic قَضَاء (qaḍāʔ, “judgement; jurisdiction”).

def: s.m. (local), A long rein for leading horses
s.f. = kajak, q.v.
adv. See s.v. qaẓā.

ety: qizil, 'red,' and bāshī, fr. bāsh, 'the head'
def: s.m. A kind of Mǒg̠al soldier. (The qazal-bāshīs are considered to be the descendants of the captives given to Shaikh Haidar by Tamerlane; they wore the red caps assumed by those captives as a mark of distinction, and are considered as the best troops of the Persian armies.)
adv. See s.v. ka.
adv. = qaẓā-kār, q.v.s.v. qaẓā.
s.f. (prob. formed fr. qaẓā), The functions or office of a qāẓī or judge.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُضَات (quzāt), from Arabic قُضَاة (quḍāh).

ety: for A. قضأة, v.n. fr. قضئي 'to be sullied'
def: s.f. Corruption; sully, tarnish, stain; defect, blemish, vice, disgrace; — a quarrel (cf. qaẓiya); — gibberish.
s.m. pl. (of qaẓiya, q.v.), Disputes, quarrels, &c.
adj. Crooked, &c. ( = kaj, q.v.): — kazh-dum = kaj-dum.
s.f. = kajak, q.v.

ety: for A. قاضٍ qāẓiṉ for qāẓiyuṉ, act. part. of قصي 'to judge, to decree,' &c.
def: s.m. A judge; a (Muhammadan) judge or magistrate (who passes sentence in all cases of law, religious, moral, civil, and criminal)
subs: s.m. 'The Judge of the necessities (of mankind,' i.e. God)
s.m. The chief or supreme judge; the head Qāẓī (under the British government, — an office now abolished); — qāẓī-kā piyāda, A constable or peon of the Qāẓī
(colloq.) 'Judge of Jaunpur'; a stupid fellow, a doit.

def: s.m. A long slender branch; a rod; a switch; — a small, elastic sword; — the yard (of man or ass).
adj. & s.m. Lying; false; — a liar.
adj. & s.m. Restraining (anger); — restrainer (of anger)
subs: One (or he) who restrains his anger.

ety: for A. قضية qaẓīyat, inf. n. of قضی 'to decree, appoint,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing; substance; action; fact; a declaration, proposition, decision, determination; (in Logic) a syllogism; — a history, narrative; — a dispute, debate, disputation, contention, wrangling, wrangle, quarrel, broil; litigation; a case, legal question; action at law, law-suit, cause; — death; — natura feminœ; vulva
subs: To raise a contention or quarrel, to fight
To settle, or put an end to, a dispute
s.m. One who excites quarrels; a mischief-maker
(-se), To bandy words (with); to contend, dispute, quarrel, wrangle
To take up the quarrel (of another); to interfere in the disputes (of others).

def: s.m. A great liar, a liar.
s.m. A Cossack; one of a gang of robbers, a robber, freebooter, brigand
s.m. A Cossack; one of a gang of robbers, a robber, freebooter, brigand
s.m. = قزاق qazzāq, q.v.
s.m. = قزاق qazzāq, q.v.
adj. Of or relating to a qazzāq; predatory; — s.f. The profession of a qazzāq; free-booting, brigandage.
s.f. = قزاقي qazzāqī, q.v.

r: ke, kī, kā, ke-taʼīṅ, ke-taiṅ, ko
def: = H کي की kī, fem., and oblique (masc.) forms respectively, of the postp. kā, q.v.
s.m. The esculent root of Colocasia antiquorum.
s.f. = keṅćlī, q.v.
s.m. A field (esp. one under water, or under cultivation); a meadow; a bed (in a field or garden); — a mountain; — the modern Kedār, a place of (Hindū) pilgrimage in the Himālaya mountains
subs: or kedāreśvar (˚ra + īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of Kedār,' an epithet of Śiva, as the object of worship at Kedār.

ety: Prk. केसरो, and केसरी; S. केशरः, and केशरी
def: s.m. A lion (see keśar, and keśarī).

ety: Prk. केसरो, and केसरी; S. केशरः, and केशरी
def: s.m. A lion (see keśar, and keśarī).
pron. A dialec. form of koʼī, q.v.
s.f. The cry of the peacock.
adj. Squint-eyed.
s.m. (f. -inī), A peacock.
s.m. = kekṛā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A crab; — the sign Cancer

ety: Prk. कयलओ or केलओ; S. कट्ल; fem. kelī = Prk. कयली or केली; S. कदल+इका
def: s.m. The plantain-tree, banana, Musa sapientum; — the fruit of the plantain, a plantain.
s.f. A daughter-in-law.
s.f. = keli or kel, q.v.
s.f. Play, sport, pastime, amusement; amorous dalliance; coition (cf. khel).
adv. Whither? ( = kidhar).
s.m. corr. of کناره kināra, q.v.
s.f. Skin or slough (of a snake)
subs: To be about to put on new slough (a snake)
or keṅćlī ḍālnā, To cast or shed its skin (a snake).

def: s.m. An earth-worm; a worm (bred in the body)
s.m. (f.?) = next, q.v.
s.m. Centre (of a circle, &c.); a focus; — a pole.
s.m. A kind of ebony, Diospyros tomentosa.
s.m. A species of ebony (that yields a kind of tar), Diospyros glutinosa.
adv. Whither? to what place?
s.m. = kekṛā, q.v.
s.m. (rustic or dialec.) = keʼoṛā, q.v.

ety: prob. A. قحط qaḥt̤ + S. aff. अ + कः (with w inserted)
def: adj. (rustic) Famine-stricken, famished, starving ( = qaḥt̤-zada).
s.f. = keʼoṭī, q.v.
s.m. = keʼoṛā, q.v.
s.m. Name of a plant which bears a strong-scented flower, a species of Pandanus odoratissimus; the flower of this plant; — scent made from this flower.
s.f. (rustic) A mixture of different kinds of pulse.

ety: Prk. करो; S. कृतः; — i.q. kar = kā, qq.v.
def: postp. (dialec. & poetic) = kā (kī, ke), q.v.
(dialec.) postp. m. (fem. -rī, obl. -re) = ker, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = kelā, q.v.
s.m. A sapling; a tender twig.
s.m. = کيش keś, q.v.
s.m. A poetic form of keś, q.v.
s.m.f. Hair (of the head); mane (of a horse, or a lion); crest or comb (of a cock)
subs: s.f. Mutual pulling of hair, fighting by pulling each other's hair
s.m. A tress or fillet of hair.

def: s.f. Saffron, Crocus sativus; Rottlera tinctoria; — Messua ferrea (syn. nāgesar); — the weeping Nyctanthes, Nyctanthes arbor tristis; — saffron colour, yellow colour.
s.m. Mane (of a horse, or a lion); — filament of a lotus, or of any vegetable; — name of several plants and their flowers (see kesar).
s.m. A lion (see keśarī).
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Having a mane; — a lion; — name of the father of Hanumāṇ
subs: s.m. Name of a particular monkey.

def: adj. Coloured or dyed with saffron, saffron-coloured, yellow; — kesarī, s.f. Saffron colour. yellow colour
subs: (or kesariyā-) bānā, adj. = kesarī; — s.m. A saffron-coloured (or yellow) dress.

def: s.m. = keśarī, q.v.
s.f. = keśariṇī, q.v.
s.f. A lioness.
adj. Coloured or dyed with saffron, saffron-coloured, yellow; — kesarī, s.f. Saffron colour. yellow colour
subs: (or kesariyā-) bānā, adj. = kesarī; — s.m. A saffron-coloured (or yellow) dress.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having long or much or handsome hair; long-haired, hairy; — an epithet of Vishṇu or Krishṇa; a proper name
adj. Prone to infidelity; (met.) a mistress, sweetheart.
s.f. = kīsa, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = کيشي keśī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having fine or long hair; having a mane; hairy, shaggy; — a hairy person; — a lion; — an epithet of Krishṇa; — name of a demon (slain by Kr̤ishṇa).
s.f. A lock of hair on the crown of the head.
adj. & s.m. = keśī, q.v.
s.f. A woman (or girl) with much and beautiful hair; — the plant Chrysopogon aciculatus; the grass Andropogon aculeatum; — name of a courtezan of Indra's paradise.
s.m. = کيتو ketu, q.v.
s.f. A strap or thong (used in the courts) for flagellation.
adj. (dialec.) = kěttā, or kittā, or kitnā, qq.v.

ety: ke, oblique of kā, q.v.+S. स्थाने
def: postp. = ko (sign of the dat. and acc.).

ety: ke, oblique of kā, q.v.+S. स्थाने
def: postp. = ko (sign of the dat. and acc.).
s.m. = ketakī, or ketkī, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = ketā, or kitnā, qq.v.
s.f. The plant and flower Pandanus odoratissimus (syn. keʼoṛā).
(dialec.), oblique of ketā, q.v.; and = kitte or kitne, q.v.
adj. (dialec.), 1˚ = kaʼī-ek, q.v.s.v. kaʼī; — 2˚ = ketak or ketik = kitnā, q.v.
(dialec.) = kete, q.v.
s.f. corr. of ketakī or ketkī, q.v.
adv. (dialec.) = kahāṅ, q.v.
adv. (dialec.) = kahāṅ, q.v.
s.f. A kettle.
adj. (rustic, or dialec.) = kitnā, q.v.

s.m. Sign, mark; ensign, flag, banner; — any unusual or striking phenomena; a comet, meteor, falling star; — the dragon's tail or descending node (considered, in astronomy, as the ninth of the planets, and in mythology, as the body of the demon Sainhikeya)
subs: s.f. A comet; a falling star
s.m. One of the nine great divisions of the known world (the western portion or varsha of Jambu-dvīp).

def: adj. & adv. Only, simple, mere, sole, exclusively proper; peculiar; absolute; rare; all, whole; — only, simply, merely, &c.; — s.m. The doctrine of the absolute unity of spirit; the knowledge of the unity of the whole universe.
adj. & s.m. Devoted to the doctrine of the absolute unity of spirit; — one who possesses the knowledge of the universal unity; (with Jains) an Arhat.

ety: prob. fr. S. केवल; or S. केवली
def: s.f. A horoscope, nativity.
s.f. Cowitch, Dolichos pruriens; or Mucuna prurita.
= H کيوارا केवारा kewārā, s.m. dialec. forms of next, q.v.
s.m. = H کيواڙا केवाड़ा kewāṛā, s.m. = H کيواڙي केवाड़ी kewāṛī, s.f. see kiwāṛ, &c.
= H کيوار केवार kewār, s.m. dialec. forms of next, q.v.
s.m. = H کيواڙ केवाड़ kewār, s.m. = H کيواڙي केवाड़ी kewāṛī, s.f. see kiwāṛ, &c.
s.f. = H کيواڙ केवाड़ kewār, s.m. = H کيواڙا केवाड़ा kewāṛā, s.m. see kiwāṛ, &c.
s.m. A fisherman; waterman, boatman ( = khewaṭ).
s.m. A bracelet worn on the upper arm.

ety: rt. of ḵẖāʼīdan; rt. Zend kar = S. kar, fr. kr̤ī; cf. ḵẖār
def: part. Chewing, gnawing, eating (used in comp.; e.g. shakar-ḵẖā, 'Sugar-eating').
s.m. A cavity, hollow, hole, aperture; vacuity, empty space, air, ether, sky, heaven; — a cypher, zero, a dot; (in Hindū poetry) a symbol for naught (0)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Moving in the air or sky, flying; — a bird; a cloud; the sun; an aerial spirit (syn. kha-ga)
s.m. Aerial crystal; the sun-gem, moon-gem
vulg. kha-sarīr, s.m. A celestial or immortal body or form
vulg. kha-sarīrī, adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Endowed with an ethereal body, having a celestial form; — a celestial

vulg. khag, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Moving in the air or sky; — air, wind; a bird; a planet (syn. kha-ćar); — khaga-ketu, or khageś, vulg. khages (˚ga+īś˚), s.m. (f. -ā), The regent or chief of the birds, an epithet of Garuṛ, q.v.; — a species of gigantic crane (known in India as the 'adjutant')
s.m. The vault or circle of the heavens, the celestial sphere; the planisphere; — the armillary sphere; — kha-gol-vidyā, s.f. Knowledge of the celestial sphere; the science of astronomy.

ety: the second letter of the guttural class
def: s.m. The letter or sound kha.
s.m. See s.v. کهہ kha.
s.m. (Dakkhinī), A wrinkle; fold; notch; groove.
s.f. Seed of the ćangel (a plant which springs out of ruined buildings).

s.f. Knowledge, acquaintance; information, a piece of information; a notification; intelligence; an announcement, advice; news, tidings, a piece of news; report, rumour; fame; — a story, an account; — notice, care, heed
subs: lit. 'To ask after the basant,' q.v.; to be extremely ignorant or negligent (of)

(-ko), To come to know, become aware; to come to one's senses; — ḵẖabar-ě-ḵẖiẓrī, s.f. Report, sudden news; guessing what is to happen (Ḵẖiẓr was a prophet, skilled in divination; and hence, when from certain symptoms the public guess at the intentions of Government, &c., intelligence of such intentions, &c. is called. ḵẖabar-ě-ḵẖiẓrī)

adj. & s.m. Having knowledge (of), acquainted (with); informed; taking care, careful, on one's guard, watchful, cautious; — a knower, one who is acquainted (with); — an informer, spy, scout; — intj. Have a care! take care! be careful! be on (your) guard! — ḵẖabar-dār rahnā (-se), To be or continue careful (of); to be attentive (to); to keep on (one's) guard (against)
v.t. To put on (one's) guard, to forewarn, caution, give an alarm
(-se), To take care (of), be on (one's) guard (against)
s.f. Informing, getting information; — taking care, carefulness; care, charge, custody, guardianship
(-kī), To take care (of), to look (after), to guard
adj. & s.m. Intelligence-giving; — one who gives information, an informer
(-ko), To give information (to), to inform, apprize
(-kī), To be informed (of), be acquainted (with); to bear in mind; to be on the look out or alert
(-ko), To acquaint, inform, apprize, advise; to report (to)
s.m. An informer, a spy; — a guardian, protector, patron; a manager
adj. & s.m. Taking care; — one who takes care (of), or looks (after), &c. ( = ḵẖabar-gīr)
s.f. Taking care (of), looking (after), managing; taking thought (of); management, protection, care, aid, support; — informing; spying
(-kī), To look (after), to manage, conduct; to protect, care (for), to aid, support
(-kī), To seek for a trace (of), to search (for stolen property, &c.)

(-kī), To look (after), take care (of), to support; to watch (over), to guard; to take notice (of); to inquire (into), to ask (about); to seek; to look (one) up; — to be after (one), to serve (one) out, settle accounts (with)
(-kī), To have knowledge (of), to be informed (of), be apprized (of), &c.
adj. Unacquainted (with, -se); ignorant, uninformed; regardless, careless, inattentive; insensible; senseless, unconscious; stupefied; intoxicated.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خبر (xabar), from Arabic خَبَر (ḵabar).

def: adj. Full of holes or hollows (a road), rough, uneven.
s.f. (prov.) = ḵẖabar.
s.f. (rustic & colloq.), corr. of ḵẖabar, q.v.

ety: for A. خباثة, inf. n. of خبث 'to be bad'
def: s.f. Badness, corruptness, wickedness, depravity; impurity; baseness; brutality
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Left; left-handed (cf. ćap); — a left-handed person.

ety: v.n. fr. خبز 'to knead and bake'
def: s.m. A baker.

ety: prob. for khubhār, fr. S. क्षुभ्; see khubnā
def: s.m. (dialec.), Wrangling; agitation, excitement; perplexity; encumbrance; trouble, distress, anguish.

ety: prob. for khubhār, fr. S. क्षुभ्; see khubnā
def: s.m. (dialec.), Wrangling; agitation, excitement; perplexity; encumbrance; trouble, distress, anguish.

ety: fr. خبث 'to be bad or corrupt'
def: adj. & s.m. Bad, corrupt, depraved, wicked, malignant; impure, foul, filthy; — an unclean spirit, an evil or malignant spirit; — a term of reproach or abuse; vile wretch; miser, &c.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖabīs̤), Impure things.
s.f. Impurity; — a malignant woman.
s.m. Dirt, sediment; mud, mire, slime.
s.m. corr. of حبشي ḥabshī, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of خبط 'to disorder, derange,' &c.
def: s.m. Making a mistake; — madness, insanity; fatuity
subs: v.n. Madness to arise, &c. ( = ḵẖabt̤ ho-jānā), to become insane, be demented or crazed.

def: adj. Mad, insane, crazy.
s.f. Crowded state, crowdedness; crush; — squash; — a heap or mass (as of fruits, leaves, rubbish, &c.); — a ball of things fastened together

ety: perf. part. of khaćnā, q.v.; — or = Prk. खचिअओ = S. खचित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Fastened, joined, stuck; mixed, blended; combined; crowded; — set (as a jewel); — studded, set (with jewels), inlaid
subs: adj. Crowded; thick, dense; closely packed, crammed, stuffed (into); — choke-full
v.t. To crowd (into, -meṅ), to pack closely; to cram, stuff (into).

def: s.f. The sound of a spear or battle-axe; the sound of treading in clay or mire (cf. also ḵẖaćar-ḵẖaćar).
s.m. See s.v. کهہ kha.
s.m. An imitative sound; splash-splash, &c.
s.m. An imitative sound; splash-splash, &c.

ety: for H. खच्चर khaććar = S. कृशर
def: s.m. A mule

r: khaćeṛnā, khaćeṛ˚, khiṅć˚, eṛ, āṛ, āl, lā
ety: khaćeṛ˚ prob. for khiṅć˚, q.v. + eṛ = āṛ = āl or lā, caus. aff.
def: v.t. To drag, pull, to drag along ( = khaiṅćnā).
part. (f. -ā) = khaćā, q.v.
s.f. The noise made by walking in mire, &c.; splash-splash; squash-squash (syn. pać-pać).

ety: khać˚ = Prk. खच्च(इ) = S. खच्य(ते), pass. of खच्
def: v.n. To be fastened, joined, or united; to be stuck together; to be mixed, blended, or combined; to be set (as a jewel); to be studded; to be inlaid.
s.f.(?) A kite; a falcon; an eagle.
s.f. Manure, &c. ( = khāt, q.v.).
s.m. A pit, &c. ( = khāt, q.v.).
s.f. An advance (made to cultivators) for food; — the expense incurred by cultivators in preparing their ploughing apparatus.
s.m. A pit, a chasm, fissure, gap; a hollow down a hill-side, low ground between mountains.
s.m. (f. -ikā), = S کهادي खादी khādī, s.m. (f. -inī), An eater; one who borrows or uses, borrower; user; debtor.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖādim), Servants, domestics, attendants, retinue.

ety: prob. khātā or khattā, q.v.+dim. aff. nā = lā = Prk. डओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A pit (from which earth is dug to make bricks or pottery), a clay-pit (see khandānā).
s.m. The white poplar, a tree from which arrows are usually made; an arrow.
s.f. Low alluvial land fit for rice cultivation.
s.f. = khādī, q.v.
s.f. = khad-bad, q.v.
s.f. = khad-bad, q.v.
v.n. To go bad
subs: v.n. intens. of khadarnā (and the more common form).

def: s.f. Sound of boiling or bubbling (as of a pot of water, &c. on the fire)

ety: prob. onomat.; or by redupl. from S. क्ष्विड or क्ष्विद्
def: v.n. To make a boiling noise, to bubble, boil, simmer.
(inf. n. of خدّ 'to furrow'), s.m.(?) A furrow; an opening, crack (in the ground); — the cheek.
s.f. A cavity, pit, hole in the ground ( = khattā, q.v.).
s.m. (dialec.) Pit, cavity, hollow; dimple; pan (of the knee); nick, notch; — adj. Gibbous.
s.f. Chase, pursuit.
v.t. = khadeṛnā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. खेट = आखेट+causat. aff. āṛ = āl+nā; cf. the dialec. form khidaṛnā
def: v.t. To chase, hunt, pursue, give chase to; to drive away; syn. khednā; rageṛnā)
s.m.f. A sword, scimitar ( = khaṛag); — the horn of a rhinoceros ( = khāg); — a rhinoceros
subs: (-ko), To strike (a blow) with a sword; to fight with a sword.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Armed with a sword; — one who is armed with a sword, a swordsman; — a rhinoceros.
s.m. (f. -inī), = S کهادك खादक khādak, s.m. (f. -ikā), An eater; one who borrows or uses, borrower; user; debtor.
s.f. A very thick and coarse kind of cotton cloth (cf. khādar, and khāruʼā).
s.f. A kind of grass that grows in ponds.
s.m. A servant; a servant in charge of a mosque or shrine; one who has charge of a religious bequest or endowment
subs: s.m. A public teacher, a professor (of a college)
s.m. A servant who takes care of a mosque, or tomb, &c.

ety: for A. خادمة ḵẖādimat, fem. of خادم
def: s.f. A female servant.
s.m. pl. of ḵẖādim), Servants, &c.
s.f. Serving; service, servitude; condition of a servant.
s.m. The tree Mimosa catechu (the resin of which is used in medicine; see katthā)
subs: (˚ra+up˚), s.m. The tree Acacia arabica (syn. babūl).

def: v.n. = khadbadānā, q.v.

ety: for A. خدشة ḵẖadshat, fem.; fr. ḵẖadsh, inf. n. of خدش 'to scratch, to hurt'
def: s.m. Hurt (of the heart); doubt, solicitude, anxiety, apprehension, fear, alarm, danger (syn. andesha; khaṭkā).
adj. To be eaten, fit or lawful to be eaten, eatable, edible; — s.m. Food, victuals, eatables
subs: s.m. A thing to be eaten; food, &c. ( = khādya).

ety: inf. n. of خفي 'to be unperceived,' &c.
def: s.f. Concealment; — a secret.
adj. = خفه ḵẖafa, q.v.

ety: Old P. ḵẖapa; prob. = S. खष्प
def: adj. Displeased, vexed, angry
s.f. Displeasure, vexation, anger
subs: adv. With a look of displeasure or anger, angrily.

ety: inf. n. of خفق 'to move to and fro; to flutter'
def: s.m. Fluttering, palpitation (esp. as a disease of the heart); hysterics.
adj. Subject to palpitation (of the heart).
adj. Imperceptible, not apparent, obscure, occult, hidden, concealed; secret, private; low, faint, soft (as a voice, &c.); fine, small (as writing, type, &c.)
subs: adj. Imperceptible and perceptible, visible and invisible; small and large (as handwriting, &c.).

def: adj. = ḵẖafī, q.v.

ety: part. n. of خفّ 'to be light'
def: adj. Light, of little weight; of little or no consequence; of light character, frivolous, undignified; immoral; — trivial, unimportant, insignificant; small, petty (as an offence, &c.); low, slight (as a fever)
subs: v.n. To be lowered, be regarded lightly, be disgraced; to be vilified, be spoken ill of; to be put to shame, to be or become ashamed; to be affronted.

ety: for A. خفيفة ḵẖafīfat, fem. of ḵẖafīf
def: s.f. A small-cause court ( = ʻadālat-e-ḵẖafīfa).
s.m. A sort of vest worn under armour, a caftan.
s.m. An egg ( = ḵẖāya).
s.m. Horn (of a rhinoceros); spur (of a cock); tusk (of a boar); — a pillar or mound (marking the boundaries of a village).
s.m. A corr. form of kāg or kāk, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. کهہ kha.
s.m. A rhinoceros.
s.m. See khag, s.v. کهہ kha.
s.m. Prk. & old H. forms of khaḍga, q.v.; and cf. khāg.
s.m. Prk. & old H. forms of khaḍga, q.v.; and cf. khāg.

ety: rel. n. fr. ḵẖāg; or contrac. of ḵẖāya+gīna
def: s.m. Fried eggs; a kind of dish made of eggs, an omelet; a pancake, &c.
s.m. See s.v. کهہ kha.
intj. Well done! bravo! how good!
s.f. A ditch, trench, moat; a drain (cf. khāt, and khāṅwā).
s.f. Dirt; rust (of iron); filth, dust, ashes, rubbish; night-soil.

ety: act. part. n. of خوف 'to fear,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Fearing, fearful, afraid, frightened, timid, pusillanimous; — a fearer, &c.
s.m. Horsemen; cavalry; — s.f. (in Persian) A body, troop, company (of men); army host, multitude; a tribe
subs: s.m. A house or family; a noble or illustrious family.

ety: prob. Prk. खेड्डओ or खेल्लओ; S. क्रीडकः
def: s.m. A calf; a young bullock, a steer.
adj. & s.f. Marked with many moles (a woman); — a woman having many moles (a great beauty).
adv. Many; much; very, extremely.

ety: for A. خيمة ḵẖaimat; fr. خيم
def: s.m. A tent, pavilion

ety: act. part. n. of خون 'to be unfaithful,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Unfaithful, treacherous, perfidious; deceitful, fraudulent; — a traitor, a treacherous man; a deceiver.

s.f. Drawing, dragging, pulling; draw, pull; attraction; drawing aside or apart (from), holding aloof (from); aversion, &c. ( = khīć, q.v.); procrastination, delay; — tenseness, tension; — scantiness; scarcity, deficiency
subs: or khaiṅć-ā-khāṅćī, or khaiṅć-ā-khaiṅć, or khaiṅć-ā-khaiṅćī, or (dialec.) khaiṅć-khameṭ, s.f. Pulling and hauling; stretching and straining; struggle, contention, scuffle.

def: v.t. = khiṅćānā = khićānā, q.v.

ety: khaiṅć˚ = Prk. कच्छ(इ) = S. क्रक्ष्य(ति), future of rt. कृष् (but used in the sense of the pres.)
def: v.t. To draw, drag, pull; to attract, to draw in, suck in, absorb (gen. khaiṅć-lenā); to draw out, to stretch; to extract; to pull off, strip off (the skin, &c.); to draw tight, to tighten; to hang (on a cross, or a gibbet; — gen. khaiṅć-denā); — to draw away or aside (from), to hold aloof ( = khićnā, q.v.); to withdraw, withhold; — to draw (a sword, or a bill, or a line or figure); to delineate, to sketch; to paint; — to drag out, to endure, suffer, bear
subs: v.t. (intens.), To pull or draw out, to extract; to extort; to pull or drag away; to drag along; to pull or draw in, to absorb, &c.
v.t. (intens.) To pull or drag out, &c.

def: v.t. = khiṅćwānā = khićwānā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of خير 'to be possessed of good'; 'to be good or well'
def: s.f. Good; goodness; benefit; good fortune, prosperity, welfare, well-being, weal; health; happiness; — adj. & adv. Good; best; well; — well; very well; it is just as well; no matter; — intj. Indeed! you don't say so!
subs: or ḵẖairuʼl-bashar, The best of men, i.e. the prophet Mohammad
The best of women, i.e. Fātima
s.m. A well-wisher; one who looks after (another's) welfare
s.f. Well-wishing; good will, good intention; benevolence; friendship
adj. & s.m. Well-wishing, benevolent, friendly, loyal; affectionate; — a well-wisher, a friend (a common signature to anonymous petitions, &c.)
adv. In good condition; well; in safety, safely, tranquilly
All passed or went off well (used where some trouble or calamity which had been anticipated did not happen)
or ḵẖair manānā (-kī), To pray for, or to desire, the good (of), to wish (one) well; to pray for the safety (of); — to seek peace
s.f. Health and prosperity; health; welfare (syn. kuśal-kshem)
It is well; is it well? is all well? what is the matter?; — it is very improper (used when one says or does a thing which ill becomes him; e.g. tum-ko ḵẖai hai).

ety: Prk. खइरं, खाइरं; — S. खदिरः, and खादिरः
def: s.m. The tree Acacia ćatechu; — catechu-extract (prepared from the Acacia catechu; — syn. katthā).
s.m. = khair, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Of the colour of catechu, brown; bluish grey; — brown colour; bluish grey colour; — a kind of pigeon.
s.m. (dialec.) The left hand; — a sprat (or a fish very much like one).
s.m. (dialec. or rustic), A calf.
s.m. = H خيرادي ḵẖairādī, s.m. corr. of ḵẖarād and ḵẖarādī, qq.v.
s.m. = H خيراد ḵẖairād, s.m. corr. of ḵẖarād and ḵẖarādī, qq.v.

ety: plur. of خيرة ḵẖairat, the fem. of ḵẖair
def: s.f. Good works, charities; charity, alms
adj. Intended for charitable purposes, to be given in charity; given, or received, in charity; charitable

ety: for A. خيرية, abst. s. fr. ḵẖair
def: s.f. Welfare, safety; happiness; — intj. Peace be to you! all hail!
s.m. Consumption, pthisis pulmonalis.
s.m. Mallow-flower; the gilliflower (esp. the yellow kind); &c. ( = gul-ě-ḵẖairū, q.v.; cf. ḵẖīrī).
s.m. The interior of the nose; the nose; the cartilaginous part of the nose; — nasal letters; — prominence, or ridge, or summit (of a mountain).
s.m. A tailor; a seamster.
s.f. The itch; cutaneous eruption; the mange
subs: (-meṅ?), The itch to break out, to have the itch
(-ko), 'The itch to seize or strike (one),' to be afflicted with the itch or mange.

def: s.m. Food; — a kind of sweetmeat (like pie-crust).
s.m. Shame, &c. ( = ḵẖijālat, q.v.).

ety: for A. خجالة, inf. n. of خجل 'to be ashamed'; — but considered by some to be a vulgar mistake for ḵẖajlat
def: s.f. Bashfulness, shame, sense of shame.
adj. Ashamed, abashed; bashful, modest.

ety: khājā, 'a kind of sweetmeat'+dim. aff. lā = rā = S. र+कः
def: s.m. = khājā, q.v.

ety: for A. خجلة, v.n. fr. خجل
def: s.f. Shame, &c. ( = ḵẖijālat); blush
subs: ḵẖijlat ḵẖānā, v.n. To feel or experience a sense of shame, to be bashful; to blush.

def: s.m. = khajūrā, q.v.

ety: Prk. खज्जूरो, or खज्जूरं; S. खर्जूरः, or खर्जूरं
def: s.f. A kind of silk cloth stained with waving marks like the traces of the old leaves on the trunk of the date-tree
subs: s.m. The juice or extract of the wild date (tāṛī), used (when in a state of fermentation) to leaven bread, and as an intoxicating liquor.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Plaited, matted; twisted, twined (as hair, mats, &c.); — s.m. The ridge of a thatched roof; — a centipede, Scolopendra (cf. kan-khajūrā).
adj. = khajūrā, q.v.
s.f. A small date-tree; — a small date.

s.f. Dust, earth; ashes; — little, precious little, none at all, nothing whatever (e.g. to kām kyā ḵẖāk hotā hogā, 'What precious little work will be doing'; ḵẖāk jāntā hai, 'He knows nothing whatever'); — intj. Out (upon)! away (with)! a fig (for)! &c. (e.g. ḵẖāk aisī zindagī-par)
subs: v.n. To go about kicking up a dust, to wander or saunter idly about
To throw dust, to raise a dust; to wander, roam; — to make a stir or commotion; to defame
v.n. Dust to be blowing; to be clouded with dust; — (fig.) to be reduced to poverty; to be disgraced
s.f. Dust of the feet; earth trodden under foot; the lowest or most worthless thing
To wander, to stray; — to deviate (from truth), to lie
s.m. A pile or mound of earth; a butt or mark of earth (for shooting arrows at); a sand-hill
To lick the dust, to humble oneself
'To sift dust,' to labour or exert oneself to no purpose, to go through laborious and fruitless toil or search; to beat the air
s.m. A receptacle or pit for dust, earth, ashes, &c.; a place where rubbish is placed, a dust-bin; — (met.) the world
(-par), To throw dust (on); to bury, to conceal (an affair, or any thing disgraceful); — to heap curses (on), to execrate; — ḵẖāk-rob, s.m. A sweeper
adj. Earth-born; produced from or made of dust
adj. 'Like dust'; base, mean, low-born, humble, lowest of the low; — s.m. Humble petitioner (used by one in speaking of himself)
s.f. Humility, self-abasement, humbleness, lowness, meanness
To throw dust and ashes on the head, to mourn, lament
(-kī), To plunder and lay waste a country, to depredate
ḵẖāk-shafā, s.f. 'Earth of healing'; sacred earth or ashes from a shrine
s.m. A person in the mint who washes the ashes to recover any particles of bullion which may have fallen; — the man who prepares earth for brick-making or pottery
v.t., and ḵẖāk-meṅ milānā, v.t. To reduce to dust and ashes, to level with the ground, to waste, ruin demolish
v.n. To mingle with the dust, to die, to perish; to be laid waste, be ruined, be demolished
v.n. To become or be reduced to dust; to be ruined ( = ḵẖāk-meṅ milnā); to moulder or pine away; to be consumed (with rage, or jealousy, &c.).

def: s.m. A plan, sketch, draft, outline, tracing, delineation; a caricature; — a kind of embroidery (worked from a pattern or paper over which net or muslin is placed to copy it)
subs: (kā), To make a tracing (of), to trace, sketch, &c.
(-kā), To caricature; to make a jest (of), to ridicule, to villify, to give a bad name (to)
ḵẖākā karnā (-kā), To make a plan or sketch (of); to make a model (of).

def: s.m. = ḵẖākā, q.v.
(prob. = ḵẖān-ḵẖān), s.m. orig. Emperor of China or Chinese Tartary; an emperor, a king.
adj. Imperial, royal; — name of a most celebrated poet of Persia.
v.n. = khaṅkhārnā, q.v.
s.f. Hurrying; bustle; labour, difficulty, trouble, trial
v.n. To hurry, to bustle to and fro; to use strenuous effort.
s.f. 1˚ a dialec. form of kākh, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of ḵẖāk, q.v.
s.m. = khaṅkhār, q.v.

s.m. A kind of rattle used in breaking in a horse (it is formed of a small board, shaped somewhat like a bleacher's beetle or mall, on each side of the flat part of which three or four rows of rattles are fixed and suspended; by an occasional shake of this, one of the horsebreaker's assistants who follows the horse with it in hand, helps to break in the horse, and to give him his proper paces).
s.f. Hurrying; bustle; labour, difficulty, trouble, trial
v.n. To hurry, to bustle to and fro; to use strenuous effort.
s.f. A hollow in a tree, &c.).

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. Prk. खुड˚ = S. खण्ड्; cf. also koṛnā
def: v.t. To scrape, scratch, rake up; to scoop out; — to make close search for, to search out.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. Prk. खुड˚ = S. खण्ड्; cf. also koṛnā
def: v.t. To scrape, scratch, rake up; to scoop out; — to make close search for, to search out.

adj. Of the dust, of ashes; earthy; earthly, terrestrial; dusty; dust-coloured; — s.f. The colour of dust or earth; — s.m. A Hindū mendicant who smears his body with the ashes of burnt cowdung; — an earthy-brown dye; cloth of the colour of dust or earth (of which the uniform of the Sikh regiments is made; hence), Sikh infantry; — an epithet of a kind of arrow; — unirrigated land (depending wholly on rain)
subs: s.m. An addled egg, wind egg; a term of reproach; bastard.

def: s.f. Ashes; calx (of metals)
subs: (-kī), To reduce to ashes, to calcine: ḵẖākistar honā, v.n. To be reduced to ashes; to be calcined.

def: adj. Covered with ashes; ashy; ash-coloured.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖākī), A denomination of Mohammadan faqīrs.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Decrepit, infirm, bent or bowed (with age); worn out (with age); — a decrepit old man; — an elder, a senior, a chief.
s.f. See s.v. ḵẖāk.
v.t. = khakhornā or khakhoṛnā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec.) = khakhornā, q.v.
s.f. A small weight (the seed of Abrus precatorius) used by goldsmiths (syn. ghuṅgćī); — a red bramble; — a small red seed used in medicine (chiefly as an eye-salve).
s.f. corr. of next, q.v.

s.m. Maternal uncle, mother's brother; — a black mole on the face (regarded as ornamental); a spot, patch (natural); an artificial spot (made of kājal, &c., for ornament, or to ward off the effects of the malignant eye)
subs: adj. & adv. Thick, close, thickly spread; — little, scarce, rare; — very few, few and far between, here and there; rarely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خال (xāl), from Arabic خال (ḵāl).

ety: inf. n. of خلع 'to pull or throw off,' &c.
def: s.m. Pulling off, stripping off, stripping; deposing, removing (from office)
subs: lit. 'He has thrown off the rein'; is unchecked or uncontrolled.

def: s.f. (m.?) A creek, bay, gulf; inlet (of the sea, or of a large river); — a canal, rivulet, watercourse, river; — a moat, trench.

ety: S. खल्लः(?); cf. ćhāl = S. शल्कं
def: s.f. Skin, hide; — bellows
subs: s.m. 'One who takes the skin off'; one who is exacting in money-matters; a skin-flint
or khāl udheṛnā, or khāl uṛānā (-kī), To take the skin off, to flay.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Low, base, mean, vile: malicious, mischievous; artful, bad, wicked; cruel; — a wicked or mischievous person, &c.; — s.m. A threshing-floor; a granary; a stock (of grain); a pit; — an entertainment; — deposit of oil, sediment, the cake that remains after the oil has been pressed out of certain seeds (esp. sesamum and sarsoṅ), oil-cake (com. khalī or kharī); — the plant Achyranthes lanata, the woolly Illecebrum or mountain knot-grass
subs: (˚la+uk˚), a.f. Low or wicked language, abuse

def: s.f. Crash (as of an earthen vessel in breaking to pieces).

ety: inf. n. of خلو 'to be empty or vacant,' &c.
def: adj. = ḵẖālī, q.v.; — s.m. A vacant place or space, vacancy, vacuity, vacuum
subs: adj. & s.m. Unfeigned, sincere; intimate, close (friendship); — sincere or un feigned friendship; warm or close attachment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلاء (xalā'), from Arabic خَلَاء (ḵalāʔ).

def: s.m. A long pole with which a boat is impelled, an oar, a helm, a rudder (syn. patwār); — any sharp piercing thing (as a needle or awl): — wind in the bowels causing acute pain; a pain that comes suddenly in the side and joints; anything that passes away by degrees or slowly; — a written document signed by a judge (confirming any mercantile transaction, &c.); — trifles, idle talk, silly discourse.
s.m. A hole, hollow, pit, cavity: low land; a ravine; a creek, &c. ( = khāl, q.v.).
s.m. A granary, &c. (see khal).

ety: for A. خالة, fem. of ḵẖāl
def: s.f. Maternal aunt, mother's sister
subs: or ḵẖālā-māmā-kā ghar, or ḵẖāna-ě-ḵẖāla, s.m. 'Aunt's house'; a place of rest and security.

ety: for A. خالة, fem. of ḵẖāl
def: s.f. See ḵẖālā.
s.m. Mud, clay, mire, filth (cf. gil).
